seo.title: Structured Output / JSON Mode implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Structured Output / JSON Mode projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist provides a technical framework for deploying LLM-based structured output pipelines. It focuses on ensuring schema adherence, minimizing parsing failures, and optimizing token costs for production-scale data generation.

items.title: Define Runtime Validation with Zod or Pydantic
items.description: Ensure every LLM response is validated against a strict runtime schema rather than relying solely on TypeScript interfaces which disappear at runtime.

items.title: Inject Field-Level Semantic Descriptions
items.description: Use .describe() in Zod or Field(description=...) in Pydantic to provide the model with explicit context for each key within the schema itself.

items.title: Enforce String Enums for Categorical Data
items.description: Replace open-ended string fields with strict enums to prevent the LLM from hallucinating variations of the same category.

items.title: Explicitly Define Nullable vs. Optional Fields
items.description: Configure the schema to distinguish between a missing key and a null value to prevent parsing errors during object instantiation.

items.title: Constraint Array Lengths
items.description: Specify minimum and maximum item counts for arrays to prevent the model from generating infinite lists or empty datasets.

items.title: Enable Provider-Specific JSON Mode
items.description: Set response_format to { 'type': 'json_object' } for OpenAI or utilize constrained decoding features in Gemini/Anthropic to force valid syntax.

items.title: Fix Temperature to 0.0
items.description: Minimize stochastic behavior by setting temperature to 0, ensuring more deterministic and predictable structured outputs.

items.title: Align System Prompt with Schema
items.description: Include a directive in the system prompt that explicitly commands the model to output only JSON and to follow the provided schema structure.

items.title: Set Appropriate Max Token Limits
items.description: Calculate the maximum possible size of the expected JSON and set max_tokens slightly above this to prevent truncated, invalid JSON strings.

items.title: Use Few-Shot Examples in Schema Format
items.description: Provide 2-3 examples of the input-to-JSON mapping within the prompt to demonstrate complex nesting or specific formatting requirements.

items.title: Implement Markdown Block Stripping
items.description: Create a pre-processor to strip '```json' and '```' tags from the LLM response before passing the string to a JSON parser.

items.title: Configure Automatic Retry Logic
items.description: Implement a 2-attempt retry loop that passes the validation error message back to the LLM to allow it to self-correct the JSON structure.

items.title: Use Partial Parsing for Streaming
items.description: If streaming output to a UI, use a library like 'partial-json-parser' to render incomplete objects without breaking the frontend.

items.title: Integrate a Schema-Aware Library
items.description: Utilize 'Instructor' or 'Vercel AI SDK' to abstract the boilerplate of mapping LLM responses to validated class instances.

items.title: Validate Business Logic Post-Parse
items.description: Run secondary validation functions after the JSON is parsed to check for logical consistency (e.g., startDate < endDate).

items.title: Benchmark Small Model Reliability
items.description: Test if GPT-4o-mini or Gemini Flash can handle your specific schema with >95% accuracy before defaulting to more expensive models.

items.title: Minify Schema Keys for High-Volume Tasks
items.description: Use shorter, abbreviated keys in the schema (e.g., 'desc' instead of 'description') to reduce output token consumption in large-scale pipelines.

items.title: Implement Semantic Caching
items.description: Cache structured outputs based on a hash of the input prompt and schema version to avoid redundant API calls for identical requests.

items.title: Monitor Token-to-Object Ratio
items.description: Track the average number of tokens required per valid object generated to identify and optimize overly verbose schemas.

items.title: Parallelize Independent Extractions
items.description: Split massive schemas into smaller, independent parallel calls if the model struggles with reasoning across too many fields simultaneously.

items.title: Log Raw and Parsed Payloads
items.description: Store the unparsed LLM string alongside the successfully validated object in your logs to facilitate debugging of edge-case parsing failures.

items.title: Track Schema Violation Rates
items.description: Set up alerts for when the rate of JSON validation failures exceeds a 2% threshold over a rolling 1-hour window.

items.title: Version the Schema Definition
items.description: Include a version identifier in your schema or metadata to manage breaking changes when updating the prompt or data structure.

items.title: Run Periodic Golden Dataset Evals
items.description: Maintain a 'golden' set of inputs and expected JSON outputs to run as a regression suite whenever the model version or prompt changes.

items.title: Audit for Hallucinated Keys
items.description: Perform periodic manual audits to ensure the LLM isn't adding fields outside the schema that the parser is silently ignoring.
